title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: * Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people * respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences * Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback * Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience * Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: * use sexualized language imagery, unwelcome sexual attention advances * Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks * Public private harassment * Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission * conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement [ignatius.pang@mq.edu.au]. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained harassing behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained harassing behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to HydraR
text: Thank interest contributing HydraR! welcome contributions community help make framework robust useful agentic research.

title: Contributing to HydraR
text: Please check existing issues opening new one. Use clear descriptive title. Provide minimal reproducible example (reprex) possible. Include information R version, OS, package versions.

title: Contributing to HydraR
text: Open new issue discuss idea starting implementation. Explain use case feature beneficial.

title: Contributing to HydraR
text: Fork repository create branch main. Follow APAF Bioinformatics coding standards. Ensure new functions documented roxygen2. Add tests new functionality tests/testthat/. Ensure devtools::check() passes without errors warnings. Run styler::style_pkg() submitting.

title: Contributing to HydraR
text: follow Tidyverse style guide. Please run styler::style_pkg() changes ensure consistency rest package.

title: Contributing to HydraR
text: Please note HydraR project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

title: Agentic Academic Researcher
text: vignette demonstrates Academic Research pattern using HydraR. define linear, multi-stage agentic workflow designed synthesize academic literature final report. pipeline comprises three agents: 1. Searcher Node: Given research topic, finds relevant academic papers. 2. Summarizer Node: Ingests raw papers extracts key findings. 3. Compiler Node: Takes extracted findings formats comprehensive literature review. pattern perfect example sequential processing pipeline without cyclic loops, node enriches state.

title: Agentic Academic Researcher
text: Initialize AgentDAG.

title: Agentic Academic Researcher
text: Mocking LLM utilizing tool search PubMed ArXiv.
code: searcher_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "Searcher", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   topic <- state$get("research_topic")    # Mock search results   papers <- list(     list(title = paste(topic, "Basics"), content = "Introduction to the topic."),     list(title = paste("Advanced", topic), content = "Complex analysis of the topic.")   )    list(     status = "SUCCESS",     output = list(       raw_papers = papers,       search_status = "Found 2 papers."     )   ) })  dag$add_node(searcher_node)

title: Agentic Academic Researcher
text: Extracts value search results.
code: summarizer_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "Summarizer", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   papers <- state$get("raw_papers")    # Mocking summarization   summaries <- lapply(papers, function(p) {     sprintf("- **%s**: Highlights - %s", p$title, p$content)   })    list(     status = "SUCCESS",     output = list(       paper_summaries = unlist(summaries)     )   ) })  dag$add_node(summarizer_node)

title: Agentic Academic Researcher
text: Drafts final markdown report.
code:
compiler_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "Compiler", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   topic <- state$get("research_topic")   summaries <- state$get("paper_summaries")    report <- paste0(     "# Literature Review: ", topic, "

",     "## Key Findings
",     paste(summaries, collapse = "
")   )    list(     status = "SUCCESS",     output = list(       final_report = report     )   ) })  dag$add_node(compiler_node)

title: Agentic Academic Researcher
text: Configure straight-pipeline: Searcher -> Summarizer -> Compiler
code: dag$set_start_node("Searcher")  dag$add_edge("Searcher", "Summarizer") dag$add_edge("Summarizer", "Compiler")  compiled_dag <- dag$compile() #> Graph compiled successfully.

title: Agentic Academic Researcher
text: Provide topic run pipeline. system successfully routed data three agents build consolidated report!
code:
initial_state <- list(   research_topic = "CRISPR Gene Editing" )  cat("Starting Literature Pipeline...
") #> Starting Literature Pipeline... result <- compiled_dag$run(initial_state = initial_state, max_steps = 5) #> Graph compiled successfully. #> [Linear] Running Node: Searcher #>    [Searcher] Executing R logic... #> [Linear] Running Node: Summarizer #>    [Summarizer] Executing R logic... #> [Linear] Running Node: Compiler #>    [Compiler] Executing R logic...  cat("
--- PIPELINE EXECUTION COMPLETE ---
") #>  #> --- PIPELINE EXECUTION COMPLETE --- cat("Search Status:", result$state$get("search_status"), "

") #> Search Status: Found 2 papers. cat(result$state$get("final_report"), "
") #> # Literature Review: CRISPR Gene Editing #>  #> ## Key Findings #> - **CRISPR Gene Editing Basics**: Highlights - Introduction to the topic. #> - **Advanced CRISPR Gene Editing**: Highlights - Complex analysis of the topic.

title: Agentic Blog Writer
text: vignette demonstrates Blog Writer pattern using HydraR. Content creation often involves multi-stage pipeline consisting brainstorming, drafting, reviewing. model DAG mix linear execution cyclic feedback. Outliner Node: Generates structured outline based topic. Drafter Node: Takes outline writes draft. also reacts feedback Editor. Editor Node: Reviews draft SEO readability. fails checks, loops back Drafter.

title: Agentic Blog Writer
text: Initialize AgentDAG.

title: Agentic Blog Writer
text: node runs beginning pipeline.
code: outliner_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "Outliner", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   topic <- state$get("blog_topic")    list(     status = "SUCCESS",     output = list(       outline = sprintf("1. Introduction matching '%s'\n2. Detailed Body\n3. Conclusion", topic)     )   ) })  dag$add_node(outliner_node)

title: Agentic Blog Writer
text: node merges outline editor feedback.
code: drafter_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "Drafter", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   outline <- state$get("outline")   feedback <- state$get("editor_feedback")    attempts <- state$get("draft_attempts")   if (is.null(attempts)) attempts <- 0   attempts <- attempts + 1    # Mocking drafting: on the second attempt, we "fix" the SEO issue   if (attempts == 1) {     draft <- "Here is a blog post based on the outline. It is very generic."   } else {     draft <- "Here is an SEO-optimized blog post with correct keywords and structure."   }    list(     status = "SUCCESS",     output = list(       blog_draft = draft,       draft_attempts = attempts     )   ) })  dag$add_node(drafter_node)

title: Agentic Blog Writer
text: Checks draft specific keywords.
code: editor_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "Editor", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   draft <- state$get("blog_draft")    if (grepl("SEO-optimized", draft)) {     list(       status = "SUCCESS",       output = list(         is_published = TRUE,         editor_feedback = "Approved for publishing."       )     )   } else {     list(       status = "SUCCESS",       output = list(         is_published = FALSE,         editor_feedback = "The draft lacks SEO optimization. Please revise."       )     )   } })  dag$add_node(editor_node)

title: Agentic Blog Writer
text: Combine linear cyclic edges.
code: dag$set_start_node("Outliner")  dag$add_edge("Outliner", "Drafter") dag$add_edge("Drafter", "Editor")  dag$add_conditional_edge(   from = "Editor",   test = function(out) {     isTRUE(out$is_published)   },   if_true = NULL, # Stop!   if_false = "Drafter" # Back to drafter )  compiled_dag <- dag$compile() #> Graph compiled successfully.

title: Agentic Blog Writer
text: DAG easily handles complex flows involving straight-processing localized feedback loops.
code:
initial_state <- list(   blog_topic = "Agentic Workflows in R" )  cat("Starting Blog Creation Engine...
") #> Starting Blog Creation Engine... result <- compiled_dag$run(initial_state = initial_state, max_steps = 10) #> Graph compiled successfully. #> [Iteration 1] Running Node: Outliner #>    [Outliner] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 2] Running Node: Drafter #>    [Drafter] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 3] Running Node: Editor #>    [Editor] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 4] Running Node: Drafter #>    [Drafter] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 5] Running Node: Editor #>    [Editor] Executing R logic...  cat("
--- BLOG PUBLICATION STATUS ---
") #>  #> --- BLOG PUBLICATION STATUS --- cat("Outline Created:
", result$state$get("outline"), "
") #> Outline Created: #>  1. Introduction matching 'Agentic Workflows in R' #> 2. Detailed Body #> 3. Conclusion cat("Total Drafts:", result$state$get("draft_attempts"), "
") #> Total Drafts: 2 cat("Final Output:
", result$state$get("blog_draft"), "
") #> Final Output: #>  Here is an SEO-optimized blog post with correct keywords and structure. cat("Editor Decision:", result$state$get("editor_feedback"), "
") #> Editor Decision: Approved for publishing.

title: Agentic Data Science Assistant
text: vignette demonstrates Data Science Assistant pattern using HydraR. machine learning workflows, hyperparameter tuning inherently iterative process. can map paradigm AgentDAG. workflow contains initial data setup phase followed cyclic model optimization phase: 1. Data Cleaner Node: Ingests prepares raw data. 2. Model Trainer Node: Mocks training model using set hyperparameters. 3. Evaluator Node: Assesses model’s accuracy. falls target threshold, routes back Trainer adjust parameters.

title: Agentic Data Science Assistant
text: Initialize AgentDAG.

title: Agentic Data Science Assistant
text: Prepares data exactly .
code: cleaner_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "DataCleaner", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   dataset <- state$get("raw_data")    # Mock cleaning   clean_data <- paste("Cleaned", dataset)    list(     status = "SUCCESS",     output = list(       clean_data = clean_data     )   ) })  dag$add_node(cleaner_node)

title: Agentic Data Science Assistant
text: Trains model iteratively. increases “epoch” “complexity” every time gets called Evaluator.
code: trainer_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "ModelTrainer", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   clean_data <- state$get("clean_data")    complexity <- state$get("model_complexity")   if (is.null(complexity)) complexity <- 1    # Increase complexity on each training loop   new_complexity <- complexity + 1    # Mocking training: accuracy improves with complexity, capping at 0.95   model_accuracy <- min(0.60 + (new_complexity * 0.10), 0.95)    list(     status = "SUCCESS",     output = list(       model_complexity = new_complexity,       model_accuracy = model_accuracy,       model_artifact = sprintf("Model_v%d", new_complexity)     )   ) })  dag$add_node(trainer_node)

title: Agentic Data Science Assistant
text: Compares accuracy user’s requirement.
code: evaluator_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "Evaluator", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   accuracy <- state$get("model_accuracy")   target <- state$get("target_accuracy")    if (accuracy >= target) {     list(       status = "SUCCESS",       output = list(         optimization_complete = TRUE,         eval_message = sprintf("Target reached: %.2f >= %.2f", accuracy, target)       )     )   } else {     list(       status = "SUCCESS",       output = list(         optimization_complete = FALSE,         eval_message = sprintf("Accuracy %.2f is below %.2f. Retraining.", accuracy, target)       )     )   } })  dag$add_node(evaluator_node)

title: Agentic Data Science Assistant
text: Configure linear start cyclic optimization loop.
code: dag$set_start_node("DataCleaner")  dag$add_edge("DataCleaner", "ModelTrainer") dag$add_edge("ModelTrainer", "Evaluator")  dag$add_conditional_edge(   from = "Evaluator",   test = function(out) {     isTRUE(out$optimization_complete)   },   if_true = NULL, # Done!   if_false = "ModelTrainer" # Needs more complexity )  compiled_dag <- dag$compile() #> Graph compiled successfully.

title: Agentic Data Science Assistant
text: Let’s test target accuracy constraint. DAG seamlessly executed data prep dropping iterative optimization loop target metric satisfied!
code:
initial_state <- list(   raw_data = "Titanic_Dataset.csv",   target_accuracy = 0.85 )  cat("Starting AutoML Pipeline...
") #> Starting AutoML Pipeline... result <- compiled_dag$run(initial_state = initial_state, max_steps = 10) #> Graph compiled successfully. #> [Iteration 1] Running Node: DataCleaner #>    [DataCleaner] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 2] Running Node: ModelTrainer #>    [ModelTrainer] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 3] Running Node: Evaluator #>    [Evaluator] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 4] Running Node: ModelTrainer #>    [ModelTrainer] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 5] Running Node: Evaluator #>    [Evaluator] Executing R logic...  cat("
--- TRAINING PIPELINE COMPLETE ---
") #>  #> --- TRAINING PIPELINE COMPLETE --- cat("Clean Data Used:", result$state$get("clean_data"), "
") #> Clean Data Used: Cleaned Titanic_Dataset.csv cat("Final Model Artifact:", result$state$get("model_artifact"), "
") #> Final Model Artifact: Model_v3 cat("Final Accuracy:", result$state$get("model_accuracy"), "
") #> Final Accuracy: 0.9 cat("Evaluation Message:", result$state$get("eval_message"), "
") #> Evaluation Message: Target reached: 0.90 >= 0.85

title: Hello World: Building an Agentic Loop with HydraR
text: vignette demonstrates construct simple “Hello World” agentic loop using HydraR framework. example, create Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) two nodes: 1. Executor node (simulating LLM external tool). 2. Validator node ensuring Executor achieved goal.

title: Hello World: Building an Agentic Loop with HydraR
text: First, load library:

title: Hello World: Building an Agentic Loop with HydraR
text: initialize AgentDAG orchestrate nodes.

title: Hello World: Building an Agentic Loop with HydraR
text: can use AgentNode subclass (AgentLogicNode downstream implementations) perform custom behaviors. , simulate agent trying guess specific word.
code: # Simulated LLM Node executor_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "Guesser", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   attempts <- state$get("attempts")   if (is.null(attempts)) attempts <- 0   attempts <- attempts + 1    # Simulate the agent guessing the right word on the 3rd attempt   guess <- if (attempts >= 3) "hello" else "hi"    list(     status = "SUCCESS",     output = list(guess = guess, attempts = attempts)   ) })  # Simulated Validator Node validator_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "Validator", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   guess <- state$get("guess")   is_valid <- (guess == "hello")   list(     status = "SUCCESS",     output = list(valid = is_valid)   ) })  dag$add_node(executor_node) dag$add_node(validator_node) dag$set_start_node("Guesser")

title: Hello World: Building an Agentic Loop with HydraR
text: create conditional transition loop back guess incorrect.
code: # Unconditional edge from Guesser to Validator dag$add_edge("Guesser", "Validator")  # Conditional edge: if not valid, loop back to Guesser dag$add_conditional_edge("Validator",   test = function(out) isTRUE(out$valid),   if_true = NULL, # Stop execution on success   if_false = "Guesser" # Loop back if not valid )

title: Hello World: Building an Agentic Loop with HydraR
text: Run DAG!
code: final_state <- dag$run(initial_state = list(attempts = 0), max_steps = 10) #> Graph compiled successfully. #> [Iteration 1] Running Node: Guesser #>    [Guesser] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 2] Running Node: Validator #>    [Validator] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 3] Running Node: Guesser #>    [Guesser] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 4] Running Node: Validator #>    [Validator] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 5] Running Node: Guesser #>    [Guesser] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 6] Running Node: Validator #>    [Validator] Executing R logic...  # View final state cat("Total Attempts:", final_state$state$get("attempts"), "\n") #> Total Attempts: 3 cat("Final Guess:", final_state$state$get("guess"), "\n") #> Final Guess: hello

title: Agentic Travel Planning: Sydney to Hong Kong
text: vignette demonstrates use HydraR orchestrate high-fidelity travel planning workflow. Unlike simple mock-based agents, use GeminiCLIDriver drive real-world logic transitions, state management, persistence complex itinerary. plan trip Sydney (SYD) Hong Kong (HKG) specific airline activity constraints.

title: Agentic Travel Planning: Sydney to Hong Kong
text: Route: Sydney Hong Kong (Departure: 29th May 2026, Return: 5th June 2026). Airline: Qantas (Required). Activities: Visit Cheung Chau Island, dine Spaghetti House, sample local Cantonese cuisine. Goal: Generate structured 3-day itinerary respects constraints.

title: Agentic Travel Planning: Sydney to Hong Kong
text: First, load library initialize GeminiCLIDriver.
code: library(HydraR)  # Initialize the Gemini CLI driver # Note: This assumes the 'gemini' CLI is installed and configured on your system. driver <- GeminiCLIDriver$new()

title: Agentic Travel Planning: Sydney to Hong Kong
text: define initial state, includes hard constraints preferences.
code: initial_state <- list(   origin = "Sydney",   destination = "Hong Kong",   departure_date = "2026-05-26",   return_date = "2026-06-01",   airline = "Qantas",   must_include = c("Cheung Chau Island", "Spaghetti House", "Local Cuisine"),   itinerary_draft = NULL,   validation_passed = FALSE )  state <- AgentState$new(initial_state)

title: Agentic Travel Planning: Sydney to Hong Kong
text: define two nodes: 1. Planner: AgentLLMNode calls Gemini generate itinerary. 2. Auditor: AgentLogicNode checks “must-include” items actually draft.

title: Agentic Travel Planning: Sydney to Hong Kong
text: use prompt_builder inject state dynamically LLM prompt.
code: planner_node <- AgentLLMNode$new(   id = "TravelPlanner",   label = "Gemini Itinerary Engine",   role = "You are a professional travel concierge specializing in premium Hong Kong travel.",   driver = driver,   prompt_builder = function(state) {     sprintf(       "Plan a 3-day trip from %s to %s.       Dates: %s to %s.       Airline: %s.       Must include: %s.       Provide a detailed itinerary in Hong Kong.",       state$get("origin"),       state$get("destination"),       state$get("departure_date"),       state$get("return_date"),       state$get("airline"),       paste(state$get("must_include"), collapse = ", ")     )   } )

title: Agentic Travel Planning: Sydney to Hong Kong
text: node uses standard R logic validate LLM’s output.
code: auditor_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(   id = "Auditor",   label = "Constraint Validator",   logic_fn = function(state) {     itinerary <- state$get("TravelPlanner") # From the previous LLM node     if (is.null(itinerary)) itinerary <- ""     must_include <- state$get("must_include")      # Simple string matching to check constraints     found <- sapply(must_include, function(x) grepl(x, itinerary, ignore.case = TRUE))     found_vec <- unlist(found)      if (all(found_vec)) {       list(         status = "SUCCESS",         output = list(validation_passed = TRUE, message = "All constraints met!")       )     } else {       missing <- must_include[!found_vec]       list(         status = "SUCCESS",         output = list(           validation_passed = FALSE,           message = paste("Missing items:", paste(missing, collapse = ", "))         )       )     }   } )

title: Agentic Travel Planning: Sydney to Hong Kong
text: link nodes add conditional loop. Auditor fails, go back Planner missing feedback.
code: dag <- AgentDAG$new() dag$add_node(planner_node) dag$add_node(auditor_node)  dag$add_edge("TravelPlanner", "Auditor")  # Conditional loop: Back to planner if validation fails dag$add_conditional_edge(   from = "Auditor",   test = function(out) isTRUE(out$validation_passed),   if_true = NULL, # END   if_false = "TravelPlanner" )  dag$set_start_node("TravelPlanner") compiled_dag <- dag$compile() #> Graph compiled successfully.

title: Agentic Travel Planning: Sydney to Hong Kong
text: can view agent’s logic directly using Mermaid.js syntax.
code:
cat("```mermaid
") ``` r cat(compiled_dag$plot(type = "mermaid")) graph TD   TravelPlanner["Gemini Itinerary Engine"]   Auditor["Constraint Validator"]   TravelPlanner --> Auditor   Auditor -- Fail --> TravelPlanner graph TD   TravelPlanner["Gemini Itinerary Engine"]   Auditor["Constraint Validator"]   TravelPlanner --> Auditor   Auditor -- Fail --> TravelPlanner cat("
```
") ## Execution  When we run the DAG, `HydraR` manages the state transitions and LLM calls. If the LLM forgets to mention "Spaghetti House", the Auditor will detect it and loop back for a correction.   ``` r # Register a checkpointer for durability checkpointer <- AgentCheckpointer$new(path = "travel_booking.duckdb") compiled_dag$set_checkpointer(checkpointer)  # Run the orchestration results <- compiled_dag$run(initial_state = initial_state, max_steps = 5)  # Display final itinerary cat(results$state$get("TravelPlanner"))

title: Agentic Travel Planning: Sydney to Hong Kong
text: workflow demonstrates HydraR provides: 1. Dynamic Prompting: Injecting complex R state LLM calls. 2. Reliability: Using logic nodes “guardrails” non-deterministic LLM output. 3. Persistence: ability checkpoint resume complex multi-step planning.

title: Agentic Personalized Shopping Assistant
text: vignette demonstrates Personalized Shopping Assistant pattern using HydraR. define agentic graph recommendation engine interacts mock user. two roles : 1. Shopper Node: Acts intelligent proxy searches catalogue based preferences. 2. User Proxy Node: Represents user. provides feedback recommended item. likes item, loop terminates. Otherwise, gives feedback requests new recommendation. models continuous interaction loop agent refines search based real-time feedback.

title: Agentic Personalized Shopping Assistant
text: Initialize AgentDAG.

title: Agentic Personalized Shopping Assistant
text: node uses user feedback update recommendations.
code: shopper_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "Shopper", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   # Extract state   request <- state$get("shopping_request")   feedback <- state$get("user_feedback")    attempts <- state$get("shopping_attempts")   if (is.null(attempts)) attempts <- 0   attempts <- attempts + 1    # Mocking catalogue search logic   recommended_item <- if (attempts == 1) {     "Generic Blue T-Shirt"   } else if (attempts == 2) {     "Premium V-Neck T-Shirt"   } else {     "Vintage Graphic T-Shirt"   }    list(     status = "SUCCESS",     output = list(       recommended_item = recommended_item,       shopping_attempts = attempts     )   ) })  dag$add_node(shopper_node)

title: Agentic Personalized Shopping Assistant
text: node simulates picky user.
code: user_proxy_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "UserProxy", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   item <- state$get("recommended_item")    if (item == "Vintage Graphic T-Shirt") {     list(       status = "SUCCESS",       output = list(         user_is_satisfied = TRUE,         user_feedback = "I love this graphic tee! I'll buy it."       )     )   } else {     list(       status = "SUCCESS",       output = list(         user_is_satisfied = FALSE,         user_feedback = sprintf("I don't like %s. Show me something with a graphic design.", item)       )     )   } })  dag$add_node(user_proxy_node)

title: Agentic Personalized Shopping Assistant
text: Configure feedback loop.
code: dag$set_start_node("Shopper")  dag$add_edge("Shopper", "UserProxy")  dag$add_conditional_edge(   from = "UserProxy",   test = function(out) {     isTRUE(out$user_is_satisfied)   },   if_true = NULL, # Done   if_false = "Shopper" # Try again )  compiled_dag <- dag$compile() #> Graph compiled successfully.

title: Agentic Personalized Shopping Assistant
text: Provide initial request observe iterations. DAG refined recommendation across 3 cycles User Proxy satisfied!
code:
initial_state <- list(   shopping_request = "I need a cool t-shirt for the weekend." )  cat("Starting Personalized Shopper...
") #> Starting Personalized Shopper... result <- compiled_dag$run(initial_state = initial_state, max_steps = 10) #> Graph compiled successfully. #> [Iteration 1] Running Node: Shopper #>    [Shopper] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 2] Running Node: UserProxy #>    [UserProxy] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 3] Running Node: Shopper #>    [Shopper] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 4] Running Node: UserProxy #>    [UserProxy] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 5] Running Node: Shopper #>    [Shopper] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 6] Running Node: UserProxy #>    [UserProxy] Executing R logic...  cat("
--- SHOPPING RESULT ---
") #>  #> --- SHOPPING RESULT --- cat("Search Attempts:", result$state$get("shopping_attempts"), "
") #> Search Attempts: 3 cat("Final Purchase:", result$state$get("recommended_item"), "
") #> Final Purchase: Vintage Graphic T-Shirt cat("User Feedback:", result$state$get("user_feedback"), "
") #> User Feedback: I love this graphic tee! I'll buy it.

title: Agentic Software Bug Assistant
text: vignette demonstrates Software Bug Assistant pattern using HydraR. scenario, two agent nodes work together resolve software bug: 1. Bug Analyzer Node: Reviews error message current code, proposes patch. 2. Test Runner Node: Simulates running test suite proposed patch. test fails, provides error traces back Bug Analyzer. classic “Generate--Test” self-healing loop.

title: Agentic Software Bug Assistant
text: Initialize AgentDAG.

title: Agentic Software Bug Assistant
text: node mocks LLM digesting bug report iterative test failures eventually produce working patch.
code: analyzer_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "Analyzer", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   # Extract state   bug_report <- state$get("bug_report")   test_feedback <- state$get("test_feedback")    attempts <- state$get("patch_attempts")   if (is.null(attempts)) attempts <- 0   attempts <- attempts + 1    # Mock behavior: It takes 2 tries to fix a Null Pointer Exception   proposed_patch <- if (attempts == 1) {     "if (x == NULL) return 0;" # Close, but maybe not quite right   } else {     "if (is.null(x)) return(0)" # Correct R syntax   }    list(     status = "SUCCESS",     output = list(       proposed_patch = proposed_patch,       patch_attempts = attempts     )   ) })  dag$add_node(analyzer_node)

title: Agentic Software Bug Assistant
text: node evaluates patch. ’ll simply check proposed patch exactly “correct R syntax” string.
code: tester_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "Tester", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   patch <- state$get("proposed_patch")    if (patch == "if (is.null(x)) return(0)") {     list(       status = "SUCCESS",       output = list(         tests_passed = TRUE,         test_feedback = "All 5 tests passed successfully."       )     )   } else {     list(       status = "SUCCESS",       output = list(         tests_passed = FALSE,         test_feedback = "Error: object 'NULL' not found. Did you mean to use 'is.null()'?"       )     )   } })  dag$add_node(tester_node)

title: Agentic Software Bug Assistant
text: structure loop. Tester conditionally loops back Analyzer upon test failure.
code: dag$set_start_node("Analyzer")  dag$add_edge("Analyzer", "Tester")  dag$add_conditional_edge(   from = "Tester",   test = function(out) {     isTRUE(out$tests_passed)   },   if_true = NULL, # End execution   if_false = "Analyzer" # Retry )  compiled_dag <- dag$compile() #> Graph compiled successfully.

title: Agentic Software Bug Assistant
text: Provide initial bug report start DAG. DAG correctly detected test failure, routed detailed feedback back analyzer, produced successful patch second attempt!
code:
initial_state <- list(   bug_report = "Function crashes when 'x' is missing." )  cat("Starting Automatic Bug Remediation...
") #> Starting Automatic Bug Remediation... result <- compiled_dag$run(initial_state = initial_state, max_steps = 10) #> Graph compiled successfully. #> [Iteration 1] Running Node: Analyzer #>    [Analyzer] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 2] Running Node: Tester #>    [Tester] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 3] Running Node: Analyzer #>    [Analyzer] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 4] Running Node: Tester #>    [Tester] Executing R logic...  cat("
--- RESOLUTION RESULT ---
") #>  #> --- RESOLUTION RESULT --- cat("Patch Attempts:", result$state$get("patch_attempts"), "
") #> Patch Attempts: 2 cat("Final Patch:", result$state$get("proposed_patch"), "
") #> Final Patch: if (is.null(x)) return(0) cat("Test Results:", result$state$get("test_feedback"), "
") #> Test Results: All 5 tests passed successfully.

title: Agentic Story Teller
text: vignette demonstrates Story Teller pattern using HydraR. workflow, two LLM agents collaborate write story: 1. Writer Agent: Drafts story based prompt incorporates feedback. 2. Reviewer Agent: Critiques draft using rubric. story meets criteria, approves draft. Otherwise, sends feedback back Writer. forms fundamental iterative critique--revise loop.

title: Agentic Story Teller
text: ’ll define AgentDAG inject two custom nodes mock LLM behavior. real-world scenario, use node_llm() AgentLogicNode bound specific LLM driver (e.g., Gemini).

title: Agentic Story Teller
text: Writer node looks current feedback () updates story_draft. ’ll mock behavior show story gets slightly better time.
code: writer_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "Writer", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   # Extract current state   current_draft <- state$get("story_draft")   iteration <- state$get("writer_iterations")   if (is.null(iteration)) iteration <- 0   feedback <- state$get("reviewer_feedback")    # Increment iteration   iteration <- iteration + 1    # Generate new draft   if (is.null(current_draft)) {     new_draft <- "Once upon a time, a brave knight went on a quest."     action_msg <- "Drafting initial story."   } else {     new_draft <- paste(current_draft, "He fought a dragon and saved the kingdom.")     action_msg <- paste("Improving story based on feedback:", feedback)   }    list(     status = "SUCCESS",     output = list(       story_draft = new_draft,       writer_iterations = iteration,       latest_action = action_msg     )   ) })  dag$add_node(writer_node)

title: Agentic Story Teller
text: Reviewer node checks story_draft. mock logic, reviewer demands least 2 iterations considers story complete.
code: reviewer_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "Reviewer", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   iteration <- state$get("writer_iterations")    if (iteration >= 2) {     # Story is approved!     list(       status = "SUCCESS",       output = list(         is_approved = TRUE,         reviewer_feedback = "This is a great story! Approved."       )     )   } else {     # Needs more work     list(       status = "SUCCESS",       output = list(         is_approved = FALSE,         reviewer_feedback = "The story lacks action. Add a dragon."       )     )   } })  dag$add_node(reviewer_node)

title: Agentic Story Teller
text: configure graph start Writer, unconditionally pass Reviewer, conditionally loop back finish.
code: dag$set_start_node("Writer")  # Unconditional transition from Writer -> Reviewer dag$add_edge("Writer", "Reviewer")  # Conditional transition from Reviewer -> Writer or END dag$add_conditional_edge(   from = "Reviewer",   test = function(out) {     # Return the boolean approval status     isTRUE(out$is_approved)   },   if_true = NULL, # Stop execution   if_false = "Writer" # Loop back to Writer )

title: Agentic Story Teller
text: compile run DAG empty initial state. set max_steps guarantee loop terminates securely (built-HydraR safeguard). can see, HydraR flawlessly orchestrated stateful communication iteration loop two mock agents.
code:
compiled_dag <- dag$compile() #> Graph compiled successfully.  cat("Starting Collaborative Writing Process...
") #> Starting Collaborative Writing Process... result <- compiled_dag$run(initial_state = list(), max_steps = 10) #> Graph compiled successfully. #> [Iteration 1] Running Node: Writer #>    [Writer] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 2] Running Node: Reviewer #>    [Reviewer] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 3] Running Node: Writer #>    [Writer] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 4] Running Node: Reviewer #>    [Reviewer] Executing R logic...  # Observe the final output final_story <- result$state$get("story_draft") total_iterations <- result$state$get("writer_iterations") feedback <- result$state$get("reviewer_feedback")  cat("--- FINAL RESULT ---
") #> --- FINAL RESULT --- cat("Total Writer Iterations:", total_iterations, "
") #> Total Writer Iterations: 2 cat("Final Feedback:", feedback, "
") #> Final Feedback: This is a great story! Approved. cat("Final Story Draft:
", final_story, "
") #> Final Story Draft: #>  Once upon a time, a brave knight went on a quest. He fought a dragon and saved the kingdom.

title: Agentic Travel Booking Concierge
text: vignette demonstrates orchestrate Travel Booking Concierge workflow using HydraR. typical travel planning AI must satisfy various constraints user’s destination, duration, crucially, budget. workflow, define two nodes: 1. Travel Planner Node: Generates proposed itinerary estimated cost based user constraints previous feedback. 2. Itinerary Validator Node: Receives proposed itinerary, checks user’s hard budget constraints, either approves demands cheaper alternative. demonstrates robust condition handling cyclic loops.

title: Agentic Travel Booking Concierge
text: Initialize AgentDAG.

title: Agentic Travel Booking Concierge
text: node mocks LLM calling flight hotel APIs, receiving feedback previous proposal expensive.
code: planner_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "Planner", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   # Read user constraints   destination <- state$get("destination")   budget <- state$get("budget")    # Check if we are iterating due to failure   attempts <- state$get("planner_attempts")   if (is.null(attempts)) attempts <- 0   attempts <- attempts + 1    # Mocking cost reduction on each attempt   # Attempt 1: $2500, Attempt 2: $1800, Attempt 3: $1200   base_cost <- 3200   proposed_cost <- base_cost - (attempts * 700)    itinerary <- sprintf("Trip to %s. Estimated Cost: $%d.", destination, proposed_cost)    list(     status = "SUCCESS",     output = list(       proposed_itinerary = itinerary,       proposed_cost = proposed_cost,       planner_attempts = attempts     )   ) })  dag$add_node(planner_node)

title: Agentic Travel Booking Concierge
text: node ensures LLM’s proposal strictly adheres numerical budget limit.
code: validator_node <- AgentLogicNode$new(id = "Validator", logic_fn = function(state, memory = NULL) {   proposed_cost <- state$get("proposed_cost")   budget <- state$get("budget")    if (proposed_cost <= budget) {     list(       status = "SUCCESS",       output = list(         is_valid = TRUE,         validation_message = "Itinerary meets budget requirements. Booking confirmed."       )     )   } else {     list(       status = "SUCCESS",       output = list(         is_valid = FALSE,         validation_message = sprintf("Proposed cost ($%d) exceeds budget ($%d). Find cheaper options.", proposed_cost, budget)       )     )   } })  dag$add_node(validator_node)

title: Agentic Travel Booking Concierge
text: define looping logic. Validator sends control back Planner is_valid FALSE.
code: dag$set_start_node("Planner")  dag$add_edge("Planner", "Validator")  dag$add_conditional_edge(   from = "Validator",   test = function(out) {     isTRUE(out$is_valid)   },   if_true = NULL, # Ends execution successfully   if_false = "Planner" # Loop back for a cheaper proposal )  compiled_dag <- dag$compile() #> Graph compiled successfully.

title: Agentic Travel Booking Concierge
text: Let’s execute DAG strict budget $1500 trip Tokyo. DAG successfully cycled back Planner node multiple times generated itinerary satisfied budget constraint!
code:
initial_state <- list(   destination = "Tokyo",   budget = 1500 )  cat("Starting Travel Booking Engine...
") #> Starting Travel Booking Engine... result <- compiled_dag$run(initial_state = initial_state, max_steps = 10) #> Graph compiled successfully. #> [Iteration 1] Running Node: Planner #>    [Planner] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 2] Running Node: Validator #>    [Validator] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 3] Running Node: Planner #>    [Planner] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 4] Running Node: Validator #>    [Validator] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 5] Running Node: Planner #>    [Planner] Executing R logic... #> [Iteration 6] Running Node: Validator #>    [Validator] Executing R logic...  # Observe the outcome cat("
--- BOOKING RESULT ---
") #>  #> --- BOOKING RESULT --- cat("Total Planning Attempts:", result$state$get("planner_attempts"), "
") #> Total Planning Attempts: 3 cat("Final Itinerary:", result$state$get("proposed_itinerary"), "
") #> Final Itinerary: Trip to Tokyo. Estimated Cost: $1100. cat("Validator Status:", result$state$get("validation_message"), "
") #> Validator Status: Itinerary meets budget requirements. Booking confirmed.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ignatius Pang. Author. Aidan Tay. Author. APAF Agentic Workflow. Maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Pang , Tay (2026). HydraR: Stateful Agentic Orchestration R (CLI-First). R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/APAF-bioinformatics/HydraR.
code: @Manual{,   title = {HydraR: Stateful Agentic Orchestration for R (CLI-First)},   author = {Ignatius Pang and Aidan Tay},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/APAF-bioinformatics/HydraR}, }

title: Stateful Agentic Orchestration for R (CLI-First)
text: HydraR lightweight, state---art orchestrator building general-purpose agentic workflows R. prioritizes CLI-native LLM interactions, hardened state management, graph-based execution (supporting Directed Acyclic Graphs iterative loops).

title: Stateful Agentic Orchestration for R (CLI-First)
text: Standard agentic frameworks often rely heavily brittle API wrappers volatile state. HydraR built durability reproducibility: - CLI-First: Directly drive high-performance CLI tools like gemini-cli, claude-code, gh copilot. - Hardened State: Implements robust state machine persistent checkpointing (DuckDB/SQLite). - Graph-Native: Design complex logic transitions loops built-validation.

title: Stateful Agentic Orchestration for R (CLI-First)
text: 📍 Graph Orchestration: Define complex agentic workflows using AgentDAG support parallel execution (furrr) conditional loops. 💾 Centralized State: AgentState provides single source truth nodes, support complex reducers history management. 🕒 Persistent Checkpointing: Resumable execution threads via Checkpointer (supporting SQLite/DuckDB). 🖥️ CLI-First Drivers: High-fidelity drivers local provider-based CLIs, ensuring tool calls environment discovery robust. 📊 Mermaid Visualization: Export agent’s logic directly Mermaid.js syntax interactive documentation. 🛡️ Validation Engine: Integrated compile-time checks undefined nodes, circular dependencies, unreachable states. 🏷️ Node Labeling: Support human-readable labels DAG nodes, independent unique IDs.

title: Stateful Agentic Orchestration for R (CLI-First)
text: can install development version GitHub:

title: Stateful Agentic Orchestration for R (CLI-First)
text: Learn use HydraR premium examples: 📍 Sydney Hong Kong Travel Planner: High-fidelity orchestration using GeminiCLIDriver book complex itinerary. 💾 Academic Research Assistant: Demonstrates literature search stateful summarization. 🛡️ Software Bug Assistant: Shows orchestrate code analysis fix suggestions.

title: Stateful Agentic Orchestration for R (CLI-First)
code: library(HydraR)  # 1. Define a simple logic node node_hello <- AgentLogicNode$new(     id = "hello_world",     logic_fn = function(state) {         input_text <- state$get("input")         list(status = "SUCCESS", output = list(message = paste("Hello", input_text)))     } )  # 2. Build the orchestrator dag <- AgentDAG$new() dag$add_node(node_hello) dag$compile()  # 3. Execute results <- dag$run(initial_state = list(input = "Hydra")) print(results$results$hello_world$output$message) # [1] "Hello Hydra"  # 4. Round-Trip Visualization dag$plot(type = "mermaid") # Outputs Mermaid syntax using node labels if provided

title: Stateful Agentic Orchestration for R (CLI-First)
text: project adheres APAF Bioinformatics standards agentic reproducibility software hardness.

Execution telemetry tracing. graph igraph. Internal graph representation. start_node String. Explicit entry point cycles. state AgentState. Centralized state object.

title: HydraR 0.1.0 (Initial Release)
text: Agentic Orchestration: Implementation AgentDAG R6 class managing complex directed workflows iterative loops (state machines). State Management: Robust AgentState system tracking data multi-step agent transitions. Provider-Agnostic Drivers: Initial support GeminiCLIDriver, ClaudeCodeDriver, CopilotCLIDriver, enabling “hot-swappable” LLM backends. High-Fidelity Validation: Integrated Auditor pattern autonomous quality control agent outputs. Persistence: DuckDB-backed AgentCheckpointer stateful execution recovery.
